non_compete.title: Post-Employment Non-Competition
non_compete.text: Employee agrees that during employment and for a period of twelve (12) months following the termination of employment for any reason, Employee will not, directly or indirectly, anywhere within the United States, engage in, be employed by, consult for, or otherwise assist any business that competes with the Company’s products or services as they exist or are in development during Employee’s employment. For purposes of this provision, a business shall be deemed a competitor if at least ten percent (10%) of its revenue is derived from products or services that compete with the Company’s core offerings in AI-enabled workflow automation platforms. Employee agrees this restriction is reasonable in scope and duration, and acknowledges that monetary damages alone may be an inadequate remedy for breach, entitling the Company to injunctive relief. This restriction is in addition to, and not in lieu of, any confidentiality or inventions assignment obligations. If any portion of this provision is deemed unenforceable, the parties authorize the court or arbitrator to modify it to the minimum extent necessary to be enforceable.

mandatory_arbitration.title: Mandatory Arbitration of Employment Disputes
mandatory_arbitration.text: As a condition of employment, Employee agrees that any and all disputes, claims, or controversies arising out of or relating to Employee’s recruitment, employment, compensation, separation, or any other interaction with the Company, including but not limited to claims under federal, state, or local statutes or common law (such as wage-and-hour, discrimination, harassment, retaliation, and tort claims), shall be resolved exclusively by final and binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association (AAA) under its Employment Arbitration Rules. The Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) shall govern this Agreement. The arbitration shall take place in San Francisco, California, before a single neutral arbitrator, and the proceedings and award shall be confidential. Each party shall bear one-half (1/2) of the arbitrator’s fees and administrative costs, and each party shall be responsible for its own attorneys’ fees except as otherwise required by statute. The arbitrator shall decide all issues of arbitrability and enforceability, including the scope, validity, and interpretation of this Agreement (delegation clause). Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may seek temporary or preliminary injunctive relief in court to protect intellectual property, trade secrets, or confidential information. Employee acknowledges that this Agreement is a material term of employment and that there is no opt-out right.

class_action_waiver.title: Class, Collective, and Representative Action Waiver (including PAGA)
class_action_waiver.text: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, Employee agrees to bring any covered claim only on an individual basis, and not as a plaintiff or class member in any purported class, collective, or representative proceeding. The parties waive the right to participate in any class action, collective action, or representative action, including but not limited to any representative action under the California Private Attorneys General Act (PAGA), Cal. Lab. Code §§ 2698 et seq. The arbitrator shall have authority only to adjudicate the individual claims of Employee and the Company. If a court or arbitrator determines that the waiver of representative or PAGA claims is unenforceable, such representative or PAGA claims shall proceed in court, while any remaining claims subject to arbitration shall be arbitrated. If any portion of this waiver is found unenforceable, it shall be severed to the extent necessary to allow enforcement of the remaining provisions.
